parameters.content:
## Create a KSeF invoice when a Sellf purchase completes

### How it works
This workflow reacts to [Sellf](https://github.com/jurczykpawel/sellf) - a self-hosted checkout platform for digital products - which sends a signed `purchase.completed` webhook for every paid order. It verifies the `X-Sellf-Signature` header, checks that the customer asked for an invoice and supplied a NIP, builds a KSeF-ready payload, and sends it to your self-hosted [KSeF Gateway](https://github.com/jurczykpawel/ksef-gateway) - a small HTTP wrapper around Poland's official e-invoice API. On success the returned `ksefNumber` is captured; on failure the error is captured so you can alert on it.

### Setup
1. Deploy a KSeF Gateway instance (Docker, Render, or StackPilot - see the project README) and note its URL. New to KSeF, or need a certificate (KSeF tokens are being phased out)? See [the main README](https://github.com/jurczykpawel/ksef-gateway#certificate-based-auth-alternative-to-tokens).
2. Open **Configuration (EDIT ME)** and fill in your gateway URL, gateway API key, seller details, and the webhook signing secret from Sellf → Settings → Webhooks.
3. Copy the **Sellf Webhook** node's Production URL into that same Sellf settings page.
4. See the red note on **Verify Signature** for one required n8n environment setting.

### Customization tips
Connect **Log Error** to Slack, email, or a database to get alerted on failed KSeF submissions.

## Receive & Verify
Accepts the Sellf webhook and checks its HMAC signature before trusting the payload.

## Acknowledge & Extract
Responds immediately, then filters for completed purchases that requested an invoice with a NIP.

## Build the Invoice
Assembles a KSeF-ready invoice payload from your seller details and the order.

## Send & Log Result
Sends the invoice to your KSeF Gateway and records the resulting KSeF number, or the error if it fails.

## Node setting required
This Code node needs `require('crypto')`. Self-hosted n8n: set `NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN=crypto` in the n8n environment, then restart.

nodes.name: Sellf Webhook
nodes.notes: Configure this webhook URL in Sellf admin panel → Settings → Webhooks. Raw Body is required so the signature check below sees the exact bytes Sellf signed.

nodes.name: Configuration (EDIT ME)
nodes.notes: Fill in your KSeF Gateway URL, seller details, and webhook signing secret before activating this workflow.

nodes.name: Verify Signature
nodes.notes: Verifies Sellf's t=...,v1=... HMAC-SHA256 header (see Sellf docs: webhook-queue/signature.ts) and re-parses the raw body for downstream nodes.

nodes.name: Respond Unauthorized
nodes.notes: Dead end: bad or missing signature. Nothing downstream trusts this payload.

nodes.name: Respond OK
nodes.notes: Responds immediately; the rest of the workflow keeps running afterwards to build and send the invoice.

nodes.name: Needs Invoice?
nodes.notes: Proceeds only when customer requested an invoice AND provided NIP. Both conditions required.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ invoiceNumber: $json.invoiceNumber, issueDate: $json.issueDate, saleDate: $json.saleDate, issuePlace: $json.issuePlace, currency: $json.currency, type: $json.type, seller: $json.seller, buyer: $json.buyer, items: $json.items, payment: $json.payment }) }}

nodes.name: Send to KSeF Gateway
nodes.notes: The gateway authenticates with KSeF internally - no auth headers needed here.

nodes.name: Extract KSeF Result
nodes.notes:
ksefNumber - save this to your DB (Supabase payment_transactions) for audit trail.
Optionally chain a Supabase node here to persist it.

nodes.name: Log Error
nodes.notes: TODO: Connect to Slack / email / Supabase audit_log to alert on failed KSeF submissions.
